title: Making a Transpiration Bag
immediate_steps: Find a living, healthy branch with plenty of leaves in direct sunlight
Place a clear plastic bag over the branch, enclosing as many leaves as possible
Tie the mouth of the bag tightly around the branch
Place a small rock inside the bag to create a low point where water will collect
Leave it for several hours as the sun causes the leaves to 'sweat' (transpire) pure water
avoid: Do not use poisonous plants (e.g., oleander)
Do not leave the bag on for more than 2-3 days (the branch will die)
